text: This is the VOA Special English Agriculture Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语农业报道。

text: A discovery in a laboratory could help lead to a new generation of drought -resistant crops.
zh: 实验室里的一项发现可能有助于培育新一代抗旱作物。

text: Drought conditions cause plants to produce a stress hormone called abscisic acid.
zh: 干旱条件会使植物产生一种叫做脱落酸的应激激素。

text: This chemical activates a set of protein molecules called receptors.
zh: 这种化学物质会激活一组被称为受体的蛋白质分子。

text: receptors.
zh: 受体。

text: These receptors then activate a series of changes to help the plant survive.
zh: 这些受体随后会引发一系列变化，帮助植物存活下来。

text: The natural reaction of the receptors is to close so -called guard cells on and inside the leaves.
zh: 受体的天然反应是关闭叶片表面及内部所谓的保卫细胞。

text: That closure decreases water loss and stops the plant from growing to save water during a drought.
zh: 这种关闭会减少水分流失，并停止植物生长，从而在干旱期间节约水分。

text: Researchers say they have discovered a way to supercharge or increase this reaction.
zh: 研究人员表示，他们发现了一种方法可以强化或增强这种反应。

text: Sean Cutler at the University of California Riverside led the team.
zh: 加州大学河滨分校的肖恩·卡特勒（Sean Cutler）领导了这个研究小组。

text: The scientists engineered abscisic acid receptors that can be turned on at will and stay on.
zh: 科学家们改造出可以随意开启并保持开启状态的脱落酸受体。

text: They tested hundreds of versions of of engineered receptor genes until they found ones that worked right together.
zh: 他们测试了数百种改造受体基因的版本，直到找到能协同工作的组合。

text: The team worked with Arabidopsis, a plant often used in experiments.
zh: 研究小组使用了拟南芥，这是一种常用于实验的植物。

text: The results recently appeared in the proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.
zh: 研究结果最近发表在《美国国家科学院院刊》上。

text: Professor Cutler says testing the new receptors in the field may take several years.
zh: 卡特勒教授表示，在田间测试这些新受体可能需要数年时间。

text: He also points out that drought is not the only cause of plant stress.
zh: 他还指出，干旱并不是植物承受压力的唯一原因。

text: What would be stressful for a crop plant is not stressful for a cactus,
zh: 对农作物来说是压力的东西，对仙人掌来说未必是压力，

text: for example, because the cactus has evolved to live in dry deserts.
zh: 因为仙人掌已经进化到能够适应干旱沙漠的生活。

text: In another development, scientists have produced a new system for analyzing genetic markers in rice plants.
zh: 另一项进展是，科学家开发出一套分析水稻基因标记的新系统。

text: A genetic marker is a DNA sequence with a known location.
zh: 基因标记是位置已知的一段DNA序列。

text: It can help scientists identify nearby genes linked to individual qualities or traits.
zh: 它可以帮助科学家找到与特定性状相关的邻近基因。

text: Anna McClung and Georgia Isinga are genetic scientists with the United States Department of Agriculture.
zh: 安娜·麦克朗（Anna McClung）和乔治亚·伊辛加（Georgia Isinga）是美国农业部的遗传学家。

text: Their new system will let researchers genetically fingerprint rice varieties and gain a better understanding of the markers.
zh: 他们的新系统将使研究人员能够对水稻品种进行基因指纹分析，从而更深入地了解这些标记。

text: Until now, breeders have been limited to using perhaps 200 markers.
zh: 迄今为止，育种者所能使用的标记大约只有200个。

text: markers.
zh: 标记。

text: But modern technology lets scientists identify differences throughout the plant's genome, its genetic map.
zh: 但现代技术让科学家能够在植物的整个基因组，也就是其遗传图谱中识别差异。

text: Ms. McClung says that means the ability to identify new genes that control biological pathways.
zh: 麦克朗女士说，这意味着有能力发现控制生物通路的新基因。

text: These pathways in the plant may control traits like yield, disease resistance, and nutritional quality.
zh: 植物中的这些通路可能控制着产量、抗病性和营养品质等性状。

text: The finding could make it easier in the future for farmers to grow designer rice, rice bred to meet their specific needs.
zh: 这一发现未来可能让农民更容易种植定制水稻，即根据自身specific需求培育的水稻。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Alex Villareal.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语，我是亚历克斯·维拉里尔（Alex Villareal）。

text: Read, listen, and learn English and much more with our programs and and activities at voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: 欢迎访问voaspecialenglish.com，通过我们的节目和活动阅读、收听并学习英语及更多内容。
